revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{神社| 名称=多爲神社| 画像=| 所在地=岐阜県関市西田原1615| 祭神=大須禰比売命| 社格=旧郷社| 創建=不明| 本殿=| 例祭=4月15...'
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=多爲神社| 画像=| 所在地=岐阜県関市西田原1615| 祭神=大須禰比売命| 社格=旧郷社| 創建=不明| 本殿=| 例祭=4月15日| 神事=| }} '''多爲神社'''（たいじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[関市]]にある[[神社]]。[[美濃国]][[加茂郡 (岐阜県)|加茂郡]][[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の多爲神社である。
多爲神社のある関市西田原は、かつての加茂郡田原村である。[[1950年]]（昭和25年）に[[武儀郡]]関町に編入され市制を施行、関市となっている。
現在の社殿は、[[天保]]年間以降の再建である。
== 祭神 == *大須禰比売命 **[[比売神]]、[[アメノホヒ|天之穂日命]]と推測されるが、詳細は不明
== 所在地 == *岐阜県関市西田原1615
== 交通機関 == *[[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]]関鵜沼線、または[[関市内巡回バス]]田原コース「大杉」バス停下車、徒歩15分
== 周辺施設 == *関市立田原小学校 *[[中日本航空専門学校]]
[[Category:岐阜県の神社|たいしんしや]] [[Category:関市|たいしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

多爲神社のある関市西田原は、かつての加茂郡[[田原村 (岐阜県)|田原村]]である。[[1950年]]（昭和25年）に[[武儀郡]]関町に編入され市制を施行、関市となっている。

revision.comment: 微加筆
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 多爲神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 岐阜県関市西田原1615 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|27|13.9|N|136|56|42.0|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 大須禰比売命 |社格 = 式内小社・旧郷社 |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = [[見世棚造|流見世棚造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月15日 |神事 = }}
'''多爲神社'''（たいじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[関市]]（[[加茂郡 (岐阜県)|旧加茂郡]][[田原村 (岐阜県)|田原村]]）にある[[神社]]。[[美濃国]]加茂郡の[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]で[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。
== 祭神 == *大須禰比売命 [[女神]]らしいが詳細は不明。[[昭和]]の『[[神社明細帳]]』に初めて見え、[[明治時代]]の『神社明細帳』には祭神不詳とあり、[[大正]]10年（[[1921年]]）の『[[美濃国加茂郡誌]]』によれば[[神体]]として木像が2体あるが、古くは3体あって[[神座]]も3座あるという。
== 由緒 == 創祀年代等不明。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に国幣の小社に列し、[[国内神名帳]]の『[[美濃国神名記]]』にも「従五位下 多井明神」と見える。「多井宮」とも称され、[[寛文]]5年（[[1665年]]）に代官により神域を守護する旨の[[禁札]]が出され、[[天保]]10年（[[1839年]]）頃に社殿が腐朽し祭祀も中絶、明治5年（[[1872年]]）頃迄は隣村（大杉村）の村民により細々と維持されて来た。明治6年に郷社に列し、同40年3月27日に[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]の指定を受けた。
== 社殿 == 現在の本殿は昭和47年（[[1972年]]）の再建。
== 交通 == *[[関市内巡回バス]]中央病院・田原線（デマンドバス）「大杉」バス停下車、徒歩15分
== 周辺施設 == *関市立田原小学校 *[[中日本航空専門学校]]
== 参考文献 == *『式内社調査報告』第13巻 東山道2、皇學館大學出版部、昭和61年
{{DEFAULTSORT:たいしんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:郷社]] [[Category:関市]] {{shinto-stub}}

== 社殿 == 現在の本殿は昭和47年（[[1972年]]）の再建。
== 交通 == *[[関市内巡回バス]]中央病院・田原線（デマンドバス）「大杉」バス停下車、徒歩15分
== 周辺施設 == *関市立田原小学校 *[[中日本航空専門学校]]

revision.comment: 立花左近 がページ「[[多爲神社 (関市)]]」を「[[多為神社 (関市)]]」に移動しました: [[ノート:多爲神社 (関市)]]の議論により
revision.text:
{{改名提案|多為神社 (関市)|date=2012年10月}} {{神社 |名称 = 多爲神社 |画像 = [[File:Tai jinja hondenmae.JPG|250px|本殿前]] |所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[関市]]西田原1615 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|27|13.9|N|136|56|42.0|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 大須禰比売命 |社格 = 式内小社・旧郷社 |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = [[見世棚造|流見世棚造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月15日 |神事 = }}
'''多爲神社'''（たいじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[関市]]（[[加茂郡 (岐阜県)|旧加茂郡]][[田原村 (岐阜県)|田原村]]）にある[[神社]]。[[美濃国]]加茂郡の[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]で[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。
== 祭神 == *大須禰比売命 [[女神]]らしいが詳細は不明。[[昭和]]の『[[神社明細帳]]』に初めて見え、[[明治時代]]の『神社明細帳』には祭神不詳とあり、[[大正]]10年（[[1921年]]）の『[[美濃国加茂郡誌]]』によれば[[神体]]として木像が2体あるが、古くは3体あって[[神座]]も3座あるという。
== 由緒 == 創祀年代等不明。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に国幣の小社に列し、[[国内神名帳]]の『[[美濃国神名記]]』にも「従五位下 多井明神」と見える。「多井宮」とも称され、[[寛文]]5年（[[1665年]]）に代官により神域を守護する旨の[[禁札]]が出され、[[天保]]10年（[[1839年]]）頃に社殿が腐朽し祭祀も中絶、明治5年（[[1872年]]）頃迄は隣村（大杉村）の村民により細々と維持されて来た。明治6年に郷社に列し、同40年3月27日に[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]の指定を受けた。
== 社殿 == 現在の本殿は昭和47年（[[1972年]]）の再建。
== 交通 == *[[関市内巡回バス]]中央病院・田原線（デマンドバス）「大杉」バス停下車、徒歩15分
== 周辺施設 == *関市立田原小学校 *[[中日本航空専門学校]]
== 参考文献 == *『式内社調査報告』第13巻 東山道2、皇學館大學出版部、昭和61年
{{DEFAULTSORT:たいしんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:関市]] {{shinto-stub}}

== 祭神 == *[[大須禰比売命]] [[女神]]らしいが詳細は不明。[[昭和]]の『[[神社明細帳]]』に初めて見え、[[明治時代]]の『神社明細帳』には祭神不詳とあり、[[大正]]10年（[[1921年]]）の『[[美濃国加茂郡誌]]』によれば[[神体]]として木像が2体あるが、古くは3体あって[[神座]]も3座あるという。
== 由緒 == 創祀年代等不明。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に国幣の小社に列し、[[国内神名帳]]の『[[美濃国神名記]]』にも「従五位下 多井明神」と見える。「多井宮」とも称され、[[寛文]]5年（[[1665年]]）に代官により神域を守護する旨の[[禁札]]が出され、[[天保]]10年（[[1839年]]）頃に社殿が腐朽し祭祀も中絶、明治5年（[[1872年]]）頃迄は隣村（大杉村）の村民により細々と維持されて来た。明治6年に郷社に列し、同40年3月27日に[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]の指定を受けた。
== 社殿 == 現在の本殿は昭和47年（[[1972年]]）の再建。
== 交通 == *[[関市内巡回バス]]中央病院・田原線（デマンドバス）「大杉」バス停下車、徒歩15分
== 周辺施設 == *[[関市立田原小学校]] *[[中日本航空専門学校]]
== 参考文献 == *『式内社調査報告』第13巻 東山道2、皇學館大學出版部、昭和61年

== 祭神 == *[[大須禰比売命]] [[女神]]らしいが詳細は不明。[[昭和]]の『[[神社明細帳]]』に初めて見え、[[明治時代]]の『神社明細帳』には祭神不詳とあり、[[大正]]10年（[[1921年]]）の『[[美濃国加茂郡誌]]』によれば[[神体]]として木像が2体あるが、古くは3体あって[[神座]]も3座あるという。
== 由緒 == 創祀年代等不明。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に国幣の小社に列し、[[国内神名帳]]の『[[美濃国神名記]]』にも「従五位下 多井明神」と見える。「多井宮」とも称され、[[寛文]]5年（[[1665年]]）に代官により神域を守護する旨の[[禁札]]が出され、[[天保]]10年（[[1839年]]）頃に社殿が腐朽し祭祀も中絶、明治5年（[[1872年]]）頃迄は隣村（大杉村）の村民により細々と維持されて来た。明治6年に郷社に列し、同40年3月27日に[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]の指定を受けた。
== 社殿 == 現在の本殿は昭和47年（[[1972年]]）の再建。
== 交通 == *[[関市内巡回バス]]中央病院・田原線（デマンドバス）「大杉」バス停下車、徒歩15分

{{nihongo|'''Tai Shrine'''|多為神社 (関市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Tai Shrine'''|多為神社 (関市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Tai Shrine'''|多為神社 (関市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tai Shrine'''|多為神社 (関市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Tai Shrine'''|多為神社 (関市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{Infobox religious building | name = Tai Shrine | native_name = 多為神社 (関市) | image = Tai jinja hondenmae.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Tai Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.453812|136.944929}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Tai Shrine'''|多為神社 (関市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]].
== instance of (P31) ==

revision.comment: 19 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 多為神社 |画像 = [[File:Tai jinja hondenmae.JPG|270px|本殿前]] |所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[関市]]西田原1615 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 大須禰比売命 |社格 = [[式内小社]]・旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = 流見世棚造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=多為神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''多為神社'''（たいじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[関市]]にある[[神社]]。[[美濃国]]加茂郡の[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]で[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。
== 祭神 == *[[大須禰比売命]] [[女神]]らしいが詳細は不明。[[昭和]]の『[[神社明細帳]]』に初めて見え、[[明治時代]]の『神社明細帳』には祭神不詳とあり、[[大正]]10年（[[1921年]]）の『[[美濃国加茂郡誌]]』によれば[[神体]]として木像が2体あるが、古くは3体あって[[神座]]も3座あるという。
== 由緒 == 創祀年代等不明。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に国幣の小社に列し、[[国内神名帳]]の『[[美濃国神名記]]』にも「従五位下 多井明神」と見える。「多井宮」とも称され、[[寛文]]5年（[[1665年]]）に代官により神域を守護する旨の[[禁札]]が出され、[[天保]]10年（[[1839年]]）頃に社殿が腐朽し祭祀も中絶、明治5年（[[1872年]]）頃迄は隣村（大杉村）の村民により細々と維持されて来た。明治6年に郷社に列し、同40年3月27日に[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]の指定を受けた。
== 社殿 == 現在の本殿は昭和47年（[[1972年]]）の再建。
== 交通 == *[[関市内巡回バス]]中央病院・田原線（デマンドバス）「大杉」バス停下車、徒歩15分
== 周辺施設 == *[[関市立田原小学校]] *[[中日本航空専門学校]]
== 参考文献 == *『式内社調査報告』第13巻 東山道2、皇學館大學出版部、昭和61年
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たいしんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:関市の歴史]] [[Category:関市の建築物]]

{{translated page|ja|多為神社 (関市)|version=107302381|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 多為神社 |画像 = [[File:Tai jinja hondenmae.JPG|270px|本殿前]] |所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[関市]]西田原1615 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 大須禰比売命 |社格 = [[式内小社]]・旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = 流見世棚造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=多為神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''多為神社'''（たいじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[関市]]にある[[神社]]。[[美濃国]]加茂郡の[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]で[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。
== 祭神 == *[[大須禰比売命]] [[女神]]らしいが詳細は不明。[[昭和]]の『[[神社明細帳]]』に初めて見え、[[明治時代]]の『神社明細帳』には祭神不詳とあり、[[大正]]10年（[[1921年]]）の『[[美濃国加茂郡誌]]』によれば[[神体]]として木像が2体あるが、古くは3体あって[[神座]]も3座あるという。
== 由緒 == 創祀年代等不明。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に国幣の小社に列し、[[国内神名帳]]の『[[美濃国神名記]]』にも「従五位下 多井明神」と見える。「多井宮」とも称され、[[寛文]]5年（[[1665年]]）に代官により神域を守護する旨の[[禁札]]が出され、[[天保]]10年（[[1839年]]）頃に社殿が腐朽し祭祀も中絶、明治5年（[[1872年]]）頃迄は隣村（大杉村）の村民により細々と維持されて来た。明治6年に郷社に列し、同40年3月27日に[[神饌幣帛料供進神社]]の指定を受けた。
== 社殿 == 現在の本殿は昭和47年（[[1972年]]）の再建。
== 交通 == *[[関市内巡回バス]]中央病院・田原線（デマンドバス）「大杉」バス停下車、徒歩15分
== 周辺施設 == *[[関市立田原小学校]] *[[中日本航空専門学校]]
== 参考文献 == *『式内社調査報告』第13巻 東山道2、皇學館大學出版部、昭和61年
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
{{shinto-stub}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:関市の歴史]] [[Category:関市の建築物]]
==End Japanese==

{{translated page|ja|多為神社 (関市)|version=107302381|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 多為神社 |画像 = [[File:Tai jinja hondenmae.JPG|270px|本殿前]] |所在地 = {{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|関市|Q860647}}西田原1615 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 大須禰比売命 |社格 = {{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287}}・旧{{jalink|郷社}} |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = 流見世棚造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=多為神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''多為神社'''（たいじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|関市|Q860647}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}加茂郡の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}で{{jalink|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社}}。
== 祭神 == *{{jalink|大須禰比売命}} {{jalink|女神|Q205985}}らしいが詳細は不明。{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}の『{{jalink|神社明細帳|Q11590704}}』に初めて見え、{{jalink|明治時代}}の『神社明細帳』には祭神不詳とあり、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}10年（{{jalink|1921年|Q2162}}）の『{{jalink|美濃国加茂郡誌}}』によれば{{jalink|神体|Q327532}}として木像が2体あるが、古くは3体あって{{jalink|神座|Q11589682}}も3座あるという。
== 由緒 == 創祀年代等不明。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に国幣の小社に列し、{{jalink|国内神名帳|Q11420490}}の『{{jalink|美濃国神名記}}』にも「従五位下 多井明神」と見える。「多井宮」とも称され、{{jalink|寛文|Q1146228}}5年（{{jalink|1665年|Q7582}}）に代官により神域を守護する旨の{{jalink|禁札}}が出され、{{jalink|天保|Q1369045}}10年（{{jalink|1839年|Q7616}}）頃に社殿が腐朽し祭祀も中絶、明治5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）頃迄は隣村（大杉村）の村民により細々と維持されて来た。明治6年に郷社に列し、同40年3月27日に{{jalink|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}の指定を受けた。
== 社殿 == 現在の本殿は昭和47年（{{jalink|1972年|Q2476}}）の再建。
== 交通 == *{{jalink|関市内巡回バス|Q11655764}}中央病院・田原線（デマンドバス）「大杉」バス停下車、徒歩15分
== 周辺施設 == *{{jalink|関市立田原小学校|Q28689084}} *{{jalink|中日本航空専門学校|Q11365097}}
== 参考文献 == *『式内社調査報告』第13巻 東山道2、皇學館大學出版部、昭和61年
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
{{shinto-stub}}

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 多為神社
|画像   = [[File:Tai jinja hondenmae.JPG|270px|本殿前]]
|所在地 = {{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|関市|Q860647}}西田原1615
|位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = 大須禰比売命
|社格   = {{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}
|創建   = 不明
|本殿   = 流見世棚造
|別名   = 
|札所等 = 
|例祭   = 4月15日
|神事   = 
|地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=多為神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}}
}}
'''多為神社'''（たいじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|関市|Q860647}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}加茂郡の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}で{{jalink|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。

=== 祭神 ===
*{{jalink|大須禰比売命}}
{{jalink|女神|Q205985}}らしいが詳細は不明。{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}の『{{jalink|神社明細帳|Q11590704}}』に初めて見え、{{jalink|明治時代}}の『神社明細帳』には祭神不詳とあり、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}10年（{{jalink|1921年|Q2162}}）の『{{jalink|美濃国加茂郡誌}}』によれば{{jalink|神体|Q327532}}として木像が2体あるが、古くは3体あって{{jalink|神座|Q11589682}}も3座あるという。

=== 由緒 ===
創祀年代等不明。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に国幣の小社に列し、{{jalink|国内神名帳|Q11420490}}の『{{jalink|美濃国神名記}}』にも「従五位下 多井明神」と見える。「多井宮」とも称され、{{jalink|寛文|Q1146228}}5年（{{jalink|1665年|Q7582}}）に代官により神域を守護する旨の{{jalink|禁札}}が出され、{{jalink|天保|Q1369045}}10年（{{jalink|1839年|Q7616}}）頃に社殿が腐朽し祭祀も中絶、明治5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）頃迄は隣村（大杉村）の村民により細々と維持されて来た。明治6年に郷社に列し、同40年3月27日に{{jalink|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}の指定を受けた。

=== 社殿 ===
現在の本殿は昭和47年（{{jalink|1972年|Q2476}}）の再建。

=== 交通 ===
*{{jalink|関市内巡回バス|Q11655764}}中央病院・田原線（デマンドバス）「大杉」バス停下車、徒歩15分

=== 周辺施設 ===
*{{jalink|関市立田原小学校|Q28689084}}
*{{jalink|中日本航空専門学校|Q11365097}}

=== 参考文献 ===
*『式内社調査報告』第13巻 東山道2、皇學館大學出版部、昭和61年

'''多為神社'''（たいじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|関市|Q860647}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}加茂郡の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}で{{jalink|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。

=== 祭神 ===
*{{jalink|大須禰比売命}}
{{jalink|女神|Q205985}}らしいが詳細は不明。{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}の『{{jalink|神社明細帳|Q11590704}}』に初めて見え、{{jalink|明治時代}}の『神社明細帳』には祭神不詳とあり、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}10年（{{jalink|1921年|Q2162}}）の『{{jalink|美濃国加茂郡誌}}』によれば{{jalink|神体|Q327532}}として木像が2体あるが、古くは3体あって{{jalink|神座|Q11589682}}も3座あるという。

=== 由緒 ===
創祀年代等不明。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に国幣の小社に列し、{{jalink|国内神名帳|Q11420490}}の『{{jalink|美濃国神名記}}』にも「従五位下 多井明神」と見える。「多井宮」とも称され、{{jalink|寛文|Q1146228}}5年（{{jalink|1665年|Q7582}}）に代官により神域を守護する旨の{{jalink|禁札}}が出され、{{jalink|天保|Q1369045}}10年（{{jalink|1839年|Q7616}}）頃に社殿が腐朽し祭祀も中絶、明治5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）頃迄は隣村（大杉村）の村民により細々と維持されて来た。明治6年に郷社に列し、同40年3月27日に{{jalink|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}の指定を受けた。

=== 社殿 ===
現在の本殿は昭和47年（{{jalink|1972年|Q2476}}）の再建。

=== 交通 ===
*{{jalink|関市内巡回バス|Q11655764}}中央病院・田原線（デマンドバス）「大杉」バス停下車、徒歩15分

=== 周辺施設 ===
*{{jalink|関市立田原小学校|Q28689084}}
*{{jalink|中日本航空専門学校|Q11365097}}

=== 参考文献 ===
*『式内社調査報告』第13巻 東山道2、皇學館大學出版部、昭和61年

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 多為神社
|画像   = [[File:Tai jinja hondenmae.JPG|270px|本殿前]]
|所在地 = {{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|関市|Q860647}}西田原1615
|位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = 大須禰比売命
|社格   = {{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}
|創建   = 不明
|本殿   = 流見世棚造
|別名   = 
|札所等 = 
|例祭   = 4月15日
|神事   = 
|地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=多為神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}}
}}
'''多為神社'''（たいじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|関市|Q860647}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}加茂郡の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}で{{jalink|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。

=== 祭神 ===
*{{jalink|大須禰比売命}}
{{jalink|女神|Q205985}}らしいが詳細は不明。{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}の『{{jalink|神社明細帳|Q11590704}}』に初めて見え、{{jalink|明治時代}}の『神社明細帳』には祭神不詳とあり、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}10年（{{jalink|1921年|Q2162}}）の『{{jalink|美濃国加茂郡誌}}』によれば{{jalink|神体|Q327532}}として木像が2体あるが、古くは3体あって{{jalink|神座|Q11589682}}も3座あるという。

=== 由緒 ===
創祀年代等不明。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に国幣の小社に列し、{{jalink|国内神名帳|Q11420490}}の『{{jalink|美濃国神名記}}』にも「従五位下 多井明神」と見える。「多井宮」とも称され、{{jalink|寛文|Q1146228}}5年（{{jalink|1665年|Q7582}}）に代官により神域を守護する旨の{{jalink|禁札}}が出され、{{jalink|天保|Q1369045}}10年（{{jalink|1839年|Q7616}}）頃に社殿が腐朽し祭祀も中絶、明治5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）頃迄は隣村（大杉村）の村民により細々と維持されて来た。明治6年に郷社に列し、同40年3月27日に{{jalink|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}の指定を受けた。

=== 社殿 ===
現在の本殿は昭和47年（{{jalink|1972年|Q2476}}）の再建。

=== 交通 ===
*{{jalink|関市内巡回バス|Q11655764}}中央病院・田原線（デマンドバス）「大杉」バス停下車、徒歩15分

=== 周辺施設 ===
*{{jalink|関市立田原小学校|Q28689084}}
*{{jalink|中日本航空専門学校|Q11365097}}

=== 参考文献 ===
*『式内社調査報告』第13巻 東山道2、皇學館大學出版部、昭和61年

=== 脚注 ===

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Tai Shrine
|画像   = [[File:Tai jinja hondenmae.JPG|270px|In front of the main sanctuary]]
|所在地 = {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Seki|Q860647}} Nishitahara 1615
|位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = Ōsunehime-no-Mikoto
|社格   = {{jalink|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287}} / former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}
|創建   = Unknown
|本殿   = Nagare-mise-dana-zukuri
|別名   = 
|札所等 = 
|例祭   = April 15
|神事   = 
|地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tai Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}}
}}
'''Tai Shrine''' (たいじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Seki|Q860647}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is a shrine listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}} for Kamo District, {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}}, and its former rank was {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{jalink|Ōsunehime-no-Mikoto}}
The deity appears to be a {{jalink|goddess|Q205985}}, but details are unknown. The name first appears in a {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}}-period ''{{jalink|Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704}}''; in the Meiji-period ''Jinja Meisaichō'' the deity is listed as unknown. According to the ''Mino no Kuni Kamo Gunshi'' from {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 10 ({{jalink|1921|Q2162}}), two wooden images were present as {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}}, but in earlier times there had been three images and also three {{jalink|shinza|Q11589682}}.

=== History ===
The date of foundation is unknown. In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'', it is listed as a small state-supported shrine; in the ''{{jalink|Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11420490}}'' and ''{{jalink|Mino no Kuni Jinmyōki}}'', it appears as "Junior Fifth Rank Lower Grade, Tai Myōjin." It was also called "Tai-no-miya." In {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 5 ({{jalink|1665|Q7582}}), a prohibition notice was issued by the local magistrate to protect the sacred precinct. Around {{jalink|Tenpō|Q1369045}} 10 ({{jalink|1839|Q7616}}), shrine buildings deteriorated and rituals were interrupted; until around Meiji 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it was barely maintained by residents of a neighboring village (Ōsugi Village). In Meiji 6 it was ranked as gōsha, and on March 27 of Meiji 40 it was designated a {{jalink|shinsen heihaku-ryō kyōshin jinja}}.

=== Shrine buildings ===
The current main sanctuary was rebuilt in Shōwa 47 ({{jalink|1972|Q2476}}).

=== Access ===
*{{jalink|Seki Community Circulation Bus|Q11655764}} Chūō Byōin / Tahara Line (demand bus): get off at "Ōsugi" bus stop, 15 minutes on foot

=== Nearby facilities ===
*{{jalink|Seki Municipal Tahara Elementary School|Q28689084}}
*{{jalink|Nakanihon Air Service Technical College|Q11365097}}

=== References ===
*''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 13, Tōsandō 2, Kōgakkan University Press, 1986

=== Notes ===

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}}

'''Tai Shrine''' (たいじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Seki|Q860647}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is a shrine listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}} for Kamo District, {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}}, and its former rank was {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{jalink|Ōsunehime-no-Mikoto}}
The deity appears to be a {{jalink|goddess|Q205985}}, but details are unknown. The name first appears in a {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}}-period ''{{jalink|Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704}}''; in the Meiji-period ''Jinja Meisaichō'' the deity is listed as unknown. According to the ''Mino no Kuni Kamo Gunshi'' from {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 10 ({{jalink|1921|Q2162}}), two wooden images were present as {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}}, but in earlier times there had been three images and also three {{jalink|shinza|Q11589682}}.

=== History ===
The date of foundation is unknown. In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'', it is listed as a small state-supported shrine; in the ''{{jalink|Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11420490}}'' and ''{{jalink|Mino no Kuni Jinmyōki}}'', it appears as "Junior Fifth Rank Lower Grade, Tai Myōjin." It was also called "Tai-no-miya." In {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 5 ({{jalink|1665|Q7582}}), a prohibition notice was issued by the local magistrate to protect the sacred precinct. Around {{jalink|Tenpō|Q1369045}} 10 ({{jalink|1839|Q7616}}), shrine buildings deteriorated and rituals were interrupted; until around Meiji 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it was barely maintained by residents of a neighboring village (Ōsugi Village). In Meiji 6 it was ranked as gōsha, and on March 27 of Meiji 40 it was designated a {{jalink|shinsen heihaku-ryō kyōshin jinja}}.

=== Shrine buildings ===
The current main sanctuary was rebuilt in Shōwa 47 ({{jalink|1972|Q2476}}).

=== Access ===
*{{jalink|Seki Community Circulation Bus|Q11655764}} Chūō Byōin / Tahara Line (demand bus): get off at "Ōsugi" bus stop, 15 minutes on foot

=== Nearby facilities ===
*{{jalink|Seki Municipal Tahara Elementary School|Q28689084}}
*{{jalink|Nakanihon Air Service Technical College|Q11365097}}

=== References ===
*''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 13, Tōsandō 2, Kōgakkan University Press, 1986

=== Notes ===

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Tai Shrine
|画像   = [[File:Tai jinja hondenmae.JPG|270px|In front of the main sanctuary]]
|所在地 = {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Seki|Q860647}} Nishitahara 1615
|位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = Ōsunehime-no-Mikoto
|社格   = {{jalink|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287}} / former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}
|創建   = Unknown
|本殿   = Nagare-mise-dana-zukuri
|別名   = 
|札所等 =

|神事   =

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}}
}}
'''Tai Shrine''' (たいじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Seki|Q860647}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is a shrine listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}} for Kamo District, {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}}, and its former rank was {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{jalink|Ōsunehime-no-Mikoto}}
The deity appears to be a {{jalink|goddess|Q205985}}, but details are unknown. The name first appears in a {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}}-period ''{{jalink|Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704}}''; in the Meiji-period ''Jinja Meisaichō'' the deity is listed as unknown. According to the ''Mino no Kuni Kamo Gunshi'' from {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 10 ({{jalink|1921|Q2162}}), two wooden images were present as {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}}, but in earlier times there had been three images and also three {{jalink|shinza|Q11589682}}.

=== History ===
The date of foundation is unknown. In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'', it is listed as a small state-supported shrine; in the ''{{jalink|Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11420490}}'' and ''{{jalink|Mino no Kuni Jinmyōki}}'', it appears as "Junior Fifth Rank Lower Grade, Tai Myōjin." It was also called "Tai-no-miya." In {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 5 ({{jalink|1665|Q7582}}), a prohibition notice was issued by the local magistrate to protect the sacred precinct. Around {{jalink|Tenpō|Q1369045}} 10 ({{jalink|1839|Q7616}}), shrine buildings deteriorated and rituals were interrupted; until around Meiji 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it was barely maintained by residents of a neighboring village (Ōsugi Village). In Meiji 6 it was ranked as gōsha, and on March 27 of Meiji 40 it was designated a {{jalink|shinsen heihaku-ryō kyōshin jinja}}.

=== Shrine buildings ===
The current main sanctuary was rebuilt in Shōwa 47 ({{jalink|1972|Q2476}}).

=== Access ===
*{{jalink|Seki Community Circulation Bus|Q11655764}} Chūō Byōin / Tahara Line (demand bus): get off at "Ōsugi" bus stop, 15 minutes on foot

=== Nearby facilities ===
*{{jalink|Seki Municipal Tahara Elementary School|Q28689084}}
*{{jalink|Nakanihon Air Service Technical College|Q11365097}}

=== References ===
*''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 13, Tōsandō 2, Kōgakkan University Press, 1986

=== Notes ===

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}}
}}
'''Tai Shrine''' (たいじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Seki|Q860647}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is a shrine listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}} for Kamo District, {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}}, and its former rank was {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{jalink|Ōsunehime-no-Mikoto}}
The deity appears to be a {{jalink|goddess|Q205985}}, but details are unknown. The name first appears in a {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}}-period ''{{jalink|Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704}}''; in the Meiji-period ''Jinja Meisaichō'' the deity is listed as unknown. According to the ''Mino no Kuni Kamo Gunshi'' from {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 10 ({{jalink|1921|Q2162}}), two wooden images were present as {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}}, but in earlier times there had been three images and also three {{jalink|shinza|Q11589682}}.

=== History ===
The date of foundation is unknown. In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'', it is listed as a small state-supported shrine; in the ''{{jalink|Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11420490}}'' and ''{{jalink|Mino no Kuni Jinmyōki}}'', it appears as "Junior Fifth Rank Lower Grade, Tai Myōjin." It was also called "Tai-no-miya." In {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 5 ({{jalink|1665|Q7582}}), a prohibition notice was issued by the local magistrate to protect the sacred precinct. Around {{jalink|Tenpō|Q1369045}} 10 ({{jalink|1839|Q7616}}), shrine buildings deteriorated and rituals were interrupted; until around Meiji 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it was barely maintained by residents of a neighboring village (Ōsugi Village). In Meiji 6 it was ranked as gōsha, and on March 27 of Meiji 40 it was designated a {{jalink|shinsen heihaku-ryō kyōshin jinja}}.

=== Shrine buildings ===
The current main sanctuary was rebuilt in Shōwa 47 ({{jalink|1972|Q2476}}).

=== Access ===
*{{jalink|Seki Community Circulation Bus|Q11655764}} Chūō Byōin / Tahara Line (demand bus): get off at "Ōsugi" bus stop, 15 minutes on foot

=== Nearby facilities ===
*{{jalink|Seki Municipal Tahara Elementary School|Q28689084}}
*{{jalink|Nakanihon Air Service Technical College|Q11365097}}

=== References ===
*''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 13, Tōsandō 2, Kōgakkan University Press, 1986

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Tai Shrine
|画像   = [[File:Tai jinja hondenmae.JPG|270px|In front of the main sanctuary]]
|所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Seki|Q860647|WD=Q860647}} Nishitahara 1615
|位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = Ōsunehime-no-Mikoto
|社格   = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}
|創建   = Unknown
|本殿   = Nagare-mise-dana-zukuri
|別名   = 
|札所等 = 
|例祭   = April 15
|神事   = 
|地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tai Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}}
}}
'''Tai Shrine''' (たいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Seki|Q860647|WD=Q860647}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}. It is a shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}} for Kamo District, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōsunehime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
The deity appears to be a {{ill|lt=goddess|Q205985|WD=Q205985}}, but details are unknown. The name first appears in a {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}-period ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704}}''; in the Meiji-period ''Jinja Meisaichō'' the deity is listed as unknown. According to the ''Mino no Kuni Kamo Gunshi'' from {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1921|Q2162|WD=Q2162}}), two wooden images were present as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}}, but in earlier times there had been three images and also three {{ill|lt=shinza|Q11589682|WD=Q11589682}}.

=== History ===
The date of foundation is unknown. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'', it is listed as a small state-supported shrine; in the ''{{ill|lt=Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11420490|WD=Q11420490}}'' and ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mino no Kuni Jinmyōki|1=UNKNOWN}}'', it appears as "Junior Fifth Rank Lower Grade, Tai Myōjin." It was also called "Tai-no-miya." In {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1665|Q7582|WD=Q7582}}), a prohibition notice was issued by the local magistrate to protect the sacred precinct. Around {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616}}), shrine buildings deteriorated and rituals were interrupted; until around Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), it was barely maintained by residents of a neighboring village (Ōsugi Village). In Meiji 6 it was ranked as gōsha, and on March 27 of Meiji 40 it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinsen heihaku-ryō kyōshin jinja|1=UNKNOWN}}.

=== Shrine buildings ===
The current main sanctuary was rebuilt in Shōwa 47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}).

=== Access ===
*{{ill|lt=Seki Community Circulation Bus|Q11655764|WD=Q11655764}} Chūō Byōin / Tahara Line (demand bus): get off at "Ōsugi" bus stop, 15 minutes on foot

=== Nearby facilities ===
*{{ill|lt=Seki Municipal Tahara Elementary School|Q28689084|WD=Q28689084}}
*{{ill|lt=Nakanihon Air Service Technical College|Q11365097|WD=Q11365097}}

=== References ===
*''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 13, Tōsandō 2, Kōgakkan University Press, 1986

=== Notes ===

{{神社

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}}

'''Tai Shrine''' (たいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Seki|Q860647|WD=Q860647|1=Seki, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for Kamo District, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōsunehime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
The deity appears to be a {{ill|lt=goddess|Q205985|WD=Q205985|1=Goddess}}, but details are unknown. The name first appears in a {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}-period ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}''; in the Meiji-period ''Jinja Meisaichō'' the deity is listed as unknown. According to the ''Mino no Kuni Kamo Gunshi'' from {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1921|Q2162|WD=Q2162|1=1921}}), two wooden images were present as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, but in earlier times there had been three images and also three {{ill|lt=shinza|Q11589682|WD=Q11589682|1=shinza}}.

=== History ===
The date of foundation is unknown. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', it is listed as a small state-supported shrine; in the ''{{ill|lt=Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11420490|WD=Q11420490|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register}}'' and ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mino no Kuni Jinmyōki|1=UNKNOWN}}'', it appears as "Junior Fifth Rank Lower Grade, Tai Myōjin." It was also called "Tai-no-miya." In {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1665|Q7582|WD=Q7582|1=1665}}), a prohibition notice was issued by the local magistrate to protect the sacred precinct. Around {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616|1=1839}}), shrine buildings deteriorated and rituals were interrupted; until around Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was barely maintained by residents of a neighboring village (Ōsugi Village). In Meiji 6 it was ranked as gōsha, and on March 27 of Meiji 40 it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinsen heihaku-ryō kyōshin jinja|1=UNKNOWN}}.

=== Shrine buildings ===
The current main sanctuary was rebuilt in Shōwa 47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}}).

=== Access ===
*{{ill|lt=Seki Community Circulation Bus|Q11655764|WD=Q11655764|1=Seki Community Circulation Bus}} Chūō Byōin / Tahara Line (demand bus): get off at "Ōsugi" bus stop, 15 minutes on foot

=== Nearby facilities ===
*{{ill|lt=Seki Municipal Tahara Elementary School|Q28689084|WD=Q28689084|1=Seki Municipal Tahara Elementary School}}
*{{ill|lt=Nakanihon Air Service Technical College|Q11365097|WD=Q11365097|1=College of Naka-nippon Aviation}}

=== References ===
*''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 13, Tōsandō 2, Kōgakkan University Press, 1986

'''Tai Shrine''' (たいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Seki|Q860647|WD=Q860647|1=Seki, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for Kamo District, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōsunehime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
The deity appears to be a {{ill|lt=goddess|Q205985|WD=Q205985|1=Goddess}}, but details are unknown. The name first appears in a {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}-period ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}''; in the Meiji-period ''Jinja Meisaichō'' the deity is listed as unknown. According to the ''Mino no Kuni Kamo Gunshi'' from {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1921|Q2162|WD=Q2162|1=1921}}), two wooden images were present as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, but in earlier times there had been three images and also three {{ill|lt=shinza|Q11589682|WD=Q11589682|1=shinza}}.

=== History ===
The date of foundation is unknown. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', it is listed as a small state-supported shrine; in the ''{{ill|lt=Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11420490|WD=Q11420490|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register}}'' and ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mino no Kuni Jinmyōki|1=UNKNOWN}}'', it appears as "Junior Fifth Rank Lower Grade, Tai Myōjin." It was also called "Tai-no-miya." In {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1665|Q7582|WD=Q7582|1=1665}}), a prohibition notice was issued by the local magistrate to protect the sacred precinct. Around {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616|1=1839}}), shrine buildings deteriorated and rituals were interrupted; until around Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was barely maintained by residents of a neighboring village (Ōsugi Village). In Meiji 6 it was ranked as gōsha, and on March 27 of Meiji 40 it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinsen heihaku-ryō kyōshin jinja|1=UNKNOWN}}.

=== Shrine buildings ===
The current main sanctuary was rebuilt in Shōwa 47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}}).

=== Access ===
*{{ill|lt=Seki Community Circulation Bus|Q11655764|WD=Q11655764|1=Seki Community Circulation Bus}} Chūō Byōin / Tahara Line (demand bus): get off at "Ōsugi" bus stop, 15 minutes on foot

=== Nearby facilities ===
*{{ill|lt=Seki Municipal Tahara Elementary School|Q28689084|WD=Q28689084|1=Seki Municipal Tahara Elementary School}}
*{{ill|lt=Nakanihon Air Service Technical College|Q11365097|WD=Q11365097|1=College of Naka-nippon Aviation}}

=== References ===
*''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 13, Tōsandō 2, Kōgakkan University Press, 1986

'''Tai Shrine''' (たいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Seki|Q860647|WD=Q860647|1=Seki, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for Kamo District, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōsunehime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
The deity appears to be a {{ill|lt=goddess|Q205985|WD=Q205985|1=Goddess}}, but details are unknown. The name first appears in a {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}-period ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}''; in the Meiji-period ''Jinja Meisaichō'' the deity is listed as unknown. According to the ''Mino no Kuni Kamo Gunshi'' from {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1921|Q2162|WD=Q2162|1=1921}}), two wooden images were present as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, but in earlier times there had been three images and also three {{ill|lt=shinza|Q11589682|WD=Q11589682|1=shinza}}.

=== History ===
The date of foundation is unknown. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', it is listed as a small state-supported shrine; in the ''{{ill|lt=Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11420490|WD=Q11420490|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register}}'' and ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mino no Kuni Jinmyōki|1=UNKNOWN}}'', it appears as "Junior Fifth Rank Lower Grade, Tai Myōjin." It was also called "Tai-no-miya." In {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1665|Q7582|WD=Q7582|1=1665}}), a prohibition notice was issued by the local magistrate to protect the sacred precinct. Around {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616|1=1839}}), shrine buildings deteriorated and rituals were interrupted; until around Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was barely maintained by residents of a neighboring village (Ōsugi Village). In Meiji 6 it was ranked as gōsha, and on March 27 of Meiji 40 it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinsen heihaku-ryō kyōshin jinja|1=UNKNOWN}}.

=== Shrine buildings ===
The current main sanctuary was rebuilt in Shōwa 47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}}).

=== Access ===
*{{ill|lt=Seki Community Circulation Bus|Q11655764|WD=Q11655764|1=Seki Community Circulation Bus}} Chūō Byōin / Tahara Line (demand bus): get off at "Ōsugi" bus stop, 15 minutes on foot

=== Nearby facilities ===
*{{ill|lt=Seki Municipal Tahara Elementary School|Q28689084|WD=Q28689084|1=Seki Municipal Tahara Elementary School}}
*{{ill|lt=Nakanihon Air Service Technical College|Q11365097|WD=Q11365097|1=College of Naka-nippon Aviation}}

=== References ===
*''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 13, Tōsandō 2, Kōgakkan University Press, 1986

The name first appears in a {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}-period ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}''; in the Meiji-period ''Jinja Meisaichō'' the deity is listed as unknown. According to the ''Mino no Kuni Kamo Gunshi'' from {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1921|Q2162|WD=Q2162|1=1921}}), two wooden images were present as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, but in earlier times there had been three images and also three {{ill|lt=shinza|Q11589682|WD=Q11589682|1=shinza}}.

=== History ===
The date of foundation is unknown. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', it is listed as a small state-supported shrine; in the ''{{ill|lt=Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11420490|WD=Q11420490|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register}}'' and ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mino no Kuni Jinmyōki|1=UNKNOWN}}'', it appears as "Junior Fifth Rank Lower Grade, Tai Myōjin." It was also called "Tai-no-miya." In {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1665|Q7582|WD=Q7582|1=1665}}), a prohibition notice was issued by the local magistrate to protect the sacred precinct. Around {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616|1=1839}}), shrine buildings deteriorated and rituals were interrupted; until around Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was barely maintained by residents of a neighboring village (Ōsugi Village). In Meiji 6 it was ranked as gōsha, and on March 27 of Meiji 40 it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinsen heihaku-ryō kyōshin jinja|1=UNKNOWN}}.

=== Shrine buildings ===
The current main sanctuary was rebuilt in Shōwa 47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}}).

=== Access ===
*{{ill|lt=Seki Community Circulation Bus|Q11655764|WD=Q11655764|1=Seki Community Circulation Bus}} Chūō Byōin / Tahara Line (demand bus): get off at "Ōsugi" bus stop, 15 minutes on foot

Around {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616|1=1839}}), shrine buildings deteriorated and rituals were interrupted; until around Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was barely maintained by residents of a neighboring village (Ōsugi Village). In Meiji 6 it was ranked as gōsha, and on March 27 of Meiji 40 it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinsen heihaku-ryō kyōshin jinja|1=UNKNOWN}}.

=== Shrine buildings ===
The current main sanctuary was rebuilt in Shōwa 47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}}).

=== Access ===
*{{ill|lt=Seki Community Circulation Bus|Q11655764|WD=Q11655764|1=Seki Community Circulation Bus}} Chūō Byōin / Tahara Line (demand bus): get off at "Ōsugi" bus stop, 15 minutes on foot

'''Tai Shrine''' (たいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Seki|Q860647|WD=Q860647|1=Seki, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for Kamo District, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōsunehime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
The deity appears to be a {{ill|lt=goddess|Q205985|WD=Q205985|1=Goddess}}, but details are unknown. The name first appears in a {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}-period ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}''; in the Meiji-period ''Jinja Meisaichō'' the deity is listed as unknown. According to the ''Mino no Kuni Kamo Gunshi'' from {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1921|Q2162|WD=Q2162|1=1921}}), two wooden images were present as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, but in earlier times there had been three images and also three {{ill|lt=shinza|Q11589682|WD=Q11589682|1=shinza}}.

=== History ===
The date of foundation is unknown. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', it is listed as a small state-supported shrine; in the ''{{ill|lt=Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11420490|WD=Q11420490|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register}}'' and ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mino no Kuni Jinmyōki|1=UNKNOWN}}'', it appears as "Junior Fifth Rank Lower Grade, Tai Myōjin." It was also called "Tai-no-miya." In {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1665|Q7582|WD=Q7582|1=1665}}), a prohibition notice was issued by the local magistrate to protect the sacred precinct. Around {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616|1=1839}}), shrine buildings deteriorated and rituals were interrupted; until around Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was barely maintained by residents of a neighboring village (Ōsugi Village). In Meiji 6 it was ranked as gōsha, and on March 27 of Meiji 40 it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinsen heihaku-ryō kyōshin jinja|1=UNKNOWN}}.

=== Shrine buildings ===
The current main sanctuary was rebuilt in Shōwa 47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}}).

=== Access ===
*{{ill|lt=Seki Community Circulation Bus|Q11655764|WD=Q11655764|1=Seki Community Circulation Bus}} Chūō Byōin / Tahara Line (demand bus): get off at "Ōsugi" bus stop, 15 minutes on foot

{{nihongo|'''Tai Shrine'''|多為神社 (関市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Tai Shrine
|画像   = [[File:Tai jinja hondenmae.JPG|270px|In front of the main sanctuary]]
|所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Seki|Q860647|WD=Q860647|1=Seki, Gifu}} Nishitahara 1615
|位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = Ōsunehime-no-Mikoto
|社格   = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
|創建   = Unknown
|本殿   = Nagare-mise-dana-zukuri
|別名   = 
|札所等 = 
|例祭   = April 15
|神事   = 
|地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tai Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}}
}}
'''Tai Shrine''' (たいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Seki|Q860647|WD=Q860647|1=Seki, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for Kamo District, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōsunehime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
The deity appears to be a {{ill|lt=goddess|Q205985|WD=Q205985|1=Goddess}}, but details are unknown. The name first appears in a {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}-period ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}''; in the Meiji-period ''Jinja Meisaichō'' the deity is listed as unknown. According to the ''Mino no Kuni Kamo Gunshi'' from {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1921|Q2162|WD=Q2162|1=1921}}), two wooden images were present as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, but in earlier times there had been three images and also three {{ill|lt=shinza|Q11589682|WD=Q11589682|1=shinza}}.

=== History ===
The date of foundation is unknown. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', it is listed as a small state-supported shrine; in the ''{{ill|lt=Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11420490|WD=Q11420490|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register}}'' and ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mino no Kuni Jinmyōki|1=UNKNOWN}}'', it appears as "Junior Fifth Rank Lower Grade, Tai Myōjin." It was also called "Tai-no-miya." In {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1665|Q7582|WD=Q7582|1=1665}}), a prohibition notice was issued by the local magistrate to protect the sacred precinct. Around {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1839|Q7616|WD=Q7616|1=1839}}), shrine buildings deteriorated and rituals were interrupted; until around Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was barely maintained by residents of a neighboring village (Ōsugi Village). In Meiji 6 it was ranked as gōsha, and on March 27 of Meiji 40 it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinsen heihaku-ryō kyōshin jinja|1=UNKNOWN}}.

=== Shrine buildings ===
The current main sanctuary was rebuilt in Shōwa 47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}}).

=== Access ===
*{{ill|lt=Seki Community Circulation Bus|Q11655764|WD=Q11655764|1=Seki Community Circulation Bus}} Chūō Byōin / Tahara Line (demand bus): get off at "Ōsugi" bus stop, 15 minutes on foot

=== Nearby facilities ===
*{{ill|lt=Seki Municipal Tahara Elementary School|Q28689084|WD=Q28689084|1=Seki Municipal Tahara Elementary School}}
*{{ill|lt=Nakanihon Air Service Technical College|Q11365097|WD=Q11365097|1=College of Naka-nippon Aviation}}

=== References ===
*''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 13, Tōsandō 2, Kōgakkan University Press, 1986

=== Notes ===
